content: read reviews/577.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/577.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/577.md.

1→# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/577.lean`
     2→
     3→Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4→without an explicit justification.
     5→
     6→Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines §1–7),
     7→[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
     8→`FormalConjectures/Util/`.
     9→
    10→---
    11→
    12→## 1. Copyright Header
    13→
    14→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
    15→  Lines 1–15 match the required Apache 2.0 header verbatim.
    16→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
    17→

    21→  `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` on line 17.
    22→- [x] No other imports are present.
    23→
    24→## 3. Module Docstring
    25→
    26→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    27→  Lines 19–31.
    28→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 577`.
    29→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link:
    30→  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/577](https://www.erdosproblems.com/577)`
    31→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
    32→  "A conjecture of Erdős and Faudree [Er90c]. Proved by Wang [Wa10]."
    33→- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [Er90c] and [Wa10] are cited with full details.
    34→
    35→## 4. Namespace
    36→
    37→- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace: `namespace Erdos577` (line 35) … `end Erdos577` (line 67).
    38→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos577`.
    39→- [x] `open` statement appears **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph` on line 33.
    40→
    41→## 5. Custom Definitions
    42→
    43→- [x] Helper definitions have LaTeX docstrings:
    44→  - `IsFourCycle` (lines 37–38): `/-- Four vertices form a $4$-cycle in $G$: ... -/`
    45→  - `fourCycleVertices` (lines 43–44): `/-- The set of vertices in a $4$-tuple. -/`
    46→- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (pure `Prop` and `Set` definitions; no classical choice).
    47→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    48→- [x] `IsFourCycle` uses `UpperCamelCase` (Prop/type). ✓
    49→- [x] `fourCycleVertices` uses `lowerCamelCase` (function returning a `Set`). ✓
    50→- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
    51→- [x] No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; not provided).
    52→
    53→## 6. Theorem Statements
    54→
    55→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 56).
    56→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_577` (`snake_case`, matching the namespace number).
    57→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
    58→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**:
    59→  `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 55).
    60→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 47–54).
    61→- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er90c]` and `[Wa10]`.
    62→
    63→### `answer()` usage
    64→
    65→The original problem is a **direct assertion** (Case 3): "If G is a graph with 4k vertices and
    66→minimum degree at least 2k then G contains k vertex-disjoint 4-cycles." It is not a yes/no
    67→question and not a "find the value" question.
    68→
    69→- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper). Correct for Case 3.
    70→- [x] Quantifiers are in the theorem body (using `∀`), not as theorem parameters. Correct.
    71→
    72→**Formalization accuracy:** The source at erdosproblems.com/577 states: "If G is a graph with 4k
    73→vertices and minimum degree at least 2k then G contains k vertex-disjoint 4-cycles." The
    74→formalization correctly captures:
    75→- Vertex set `Fin (4 * k)` for 4k vertices.
    76→- Minimum degree condition `∀ v, 2 * k ≤ G.degree v`.
    77→- Existence of `k` vertex-disjoint 4-cycles via `Fin k → ...` with `IsFourCycle` and `Disjoint`.
    78→- `IsFourCycle` correctly requires pairwise distinct vertices and cyclic adjacency a~b~c~d~a.
    79→- `fourCycleVertices` correctly extracts the set {a, b, c, d} for disjointness checking.
    80→
    81→## 7. Category Attribute
    82→

    84→- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (proved by Wang [Wa10] but not formally verified).
    85→- [x] N/A — not `research open`, so the `sorry`-free proof rule does not apply.
    86→
    87→## 8. AMS Attribute
    88→
    89→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
    90→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (combinatorics).
    91→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
    92→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    93→- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    94→

    97→- [x] Uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` form (lines 64–65), which is acceptable per the checklist.

   102→

   104→
   105→- [x] N/A — No variants present. The problem has a single statement.
   106→
   107→## 11. Code Quality
   108→
   109→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
   110→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `∀`, `∃`, `≠`, `≤`, `→`, `ℕ`.
   111→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
   112→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
   113→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
   114→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
   115→
   116→## 12. Build Verification
   117→
   118→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review (requires build environment).
   119→- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.
   120→
   121→---
   122→
   123→**Verdict:** All style and content checks pass. The formalization accurately captures the
   124→Erdős–Faudree conjecture (proved by Wang) as stated at erdosproblems.com/577. Build verification

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/577.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines §1–7),
[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
`FormalConjectures/Util/`.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
  Lines 1–15 match the required Apache 2.0 header verbatim.
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` on line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
  Lines 19–31.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 577`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link:
  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/577](https://www.erdosproblems.com/577)`
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
  "A conjecture of Erdős and Faudree [Er90c]. Proved by Wang [Wa10]."
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [Er90c] and [Wa10] are cited with full details.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace: `namespace Erdos577` (line 35) … `end Erdos577` (line 67).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos577`.
- [x] `open` statement appears **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph` on line 33.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper definitions have LaTeX docstrings:
  - `IsFourCycle` (lines 37–38): `/-- Four vertices form a $4$-cycle in $G$: ... -/`
  - `fourCycleVertices` (lines 43–44): `/-- The set of vertices in a $4$-tuple. -/`
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (pure `Prop` and `Set` definitions; no classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `IsFourCycle` uses `UpperCamelCase` (Prop/type). ✓
- [x] `fourCycleVertices` uses `lowerCamelCase` (function returning a `Set`). ✓
- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
- [x] No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; not provided).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 56).
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_577` (`snake_case`, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**:
  `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 55).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 47–54).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er90c]` and `[Wa10]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem is a **direct assertion** (Case 3): "If G is a graph with 4k vertices and
minimum degree at least 2k then G contains k vertex-disjoint 4-cycles." It is not a yes/no
question and not a "find the value" question.

- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper). Correct for Case 3.
- [x] Quantifiers are in the theorem body (using `∀`), not as theorem parameters. Correct.

**Formalization accuracy:** The source at erdosproblems.com/577 states: "If G is a graph with 4k
vertices and minimum degree at least 2k then G contains k vertex-disjoint 4-cycles." The
formalization correctly captures:
- Vertex set `Fin (4 * k)` for 4k vertices.
- Minimum degree condition `∀ v, 2 * k ≤ G.degree v`.
- Existence of `k` vertex-disjoint 4-cycles via `Fin k → ...` with `IsFourCycle` and `Disjoint`.
- `IsFourCycle` correctly requires pairwise distinct vertices and cyclic adjacency a~b~c~d~a.
- `fourCycleVertices` correctly extracts the set {a, b, c, d} for disjointness checking.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research solved`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (proved by Wang [Wa10] but not formally verified).
- [x] N/A — not `research open`, so the `sorry`-free proof rule does not apply.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` form (lines 64–65), which is acceptable per the checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included (appropriate — this is a solved problem but the repo
  doesn't include proof sketches for solved problems either).
- [x] No `native_decide` used.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] N/A — No variants present. The problem has a single statement.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `∀`, `∃`, `≠`, `≤`, `→`, `ℕ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

---

**Verdict:** All style and content checks pass. The formalization accurately captures the
Erdős–Faudree conjecture (proved by Wang) as stated at erdosproblems.com/577. Build verification
remains to be confirmed.

content.text: The review passed — all style and content checks are marked as passing, with only the build verification items unchecked (which require a build environment and aren't content issues). No changes needed.
